title: Looking for (Genomic) Needles in a Haystack: Sparsity-Driven Search for Identifying Correlated Genetic Mutations in Cancer
venue: IEEE International Parallel & Distributed Processing Symposium (IPDPS)
authors: R Prabhu, E Vatai, B Moussad, E Jeannot, R Anandakrishnan, W Feng, M Wahib
summary: Scales sparsity-driven search for identifying correlated genetic mutations in cancer.
abstract: This work studies how to identify correlated genetic mutation combinations in cancer by scaling sparsity-driven search over an irregular, combinatorial space. The systems focus is on hierarchical MPI work stealing, load balance, and full-scale HPC execution for scientific discovery.

title: ResearchBot: Bridging the Gap between Academic Research and Practical Programming Communities
venue: IEEE/ACM International Workshop on Bots in Software Engineering (BotSE)
authors: S Farzanepour, S Rajeev, H Liang, R Prabhu, C Brown
summary: Explores how software bots can connect academic research with practical programming communities.
abstract: This paper studies ResearchBot, a software-engineering bot aimed at bridging academic research and practical programming communities. It sits outside my main HPC thread but reflects an interest in systems that make research more usable by practitioners.

title: Balancing Performance and Productivity: A Comparative Study of Apache Arrow vs. MPI
venue: 2025 IEEE High Performance Extreme Computing Conference (HPEC), 1–7
summary: Compares Apache Arrow and MPI through the lens of performance, productivity, and data movement in HPC workflows.
abstract: This work compares Apache Arrow and MPI as approaches for high-performance data handling, asking how performance and developer productivity trade off in real HPC workflows. It connects data layout, communication, and productivity concerns that matter for exascale scientific computing pipelines.

title: Improved 2-D Chest CT Image Enhancement with Multi-Level VGG Loss
venue: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences 9(3), 304–312
authors: A Chaturvedi, R Prabhu, M Yadav, WC Feng, G Cao
summary: Studies multi-level VGG loss for improving 2-D chest CT image enhancement.
abstract: This paper studies 2-D chest CT image enhancement using a multi-level VGG loss. It connects medical imaging, deep learning, perceptual loss design, and image-quality evaluation in a scientific ML setting.

title: On the Scalability of Computing Genomic Diversity Using SparkLeBLAST: A Feasibility Study
venue: 2024 IEEE High Performance Extreme Computing Conference (HPEC), 1–8
authors: R Prabhu, B Moussad, K Youssef, E Vatai, W Feng
summary: Studies the feasibility and scalability of SparkLeBLAST for genomic diversity analysis on HPC systems.
abstract: This paper analyzes SparkLeBLAST as a scalable pipeline for genomic diversity study, identifying performance bottlenecks and feasibility constraints for large-scale execution. The work connects bioinformatics, exascale readiness, in-memory representation, and HPC performance analysis.

title: Explainable Interactive Projections for Image Data
authors: H Han, R Faust, BFK Norambuena, R Prabhu, T Smith, S Li, C North
summary: Builds explainable interactive projection methods for image data exploration.
abstract: This paper presents explainable interactive projection techniques for image data. It connects human-in-the-loop visual analytics with feature-space manipulation and interpretable image exploration.

title: Interactive Deep Learning for Sorting Plant Images by Visual Phenotypes
authors: H Han, R Prabhu, T Smith, K Dhakal, X Wei, S Li, C North
summary: Uses interactive deep learning to sort plant images by visual phenotypes.
abstract: This work explores interactive deep learning for sorting plant images by visual phenotypes, using human feedback to shape visual similarity and projection behavior. It reflects my early work at the intersection of visualization, machine learning, and image data.
